  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Finished water production records are associated with clearwells and storage assets (registered assets). Asset-level production tracking enables capital planning, condition-based capacity analysis, an']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production record was approved by the supervising operator for MOR reporting (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements and regulatory submission deadline tracking.']
  avg_production_rate_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average finished water production flow rate during this production period, measured in Gallons per Minute (GPM). Derived from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian flow meter data. Used for hydraulic loading analysis, pump performance monitoring, and comparison against design flow rates.']
  backwash_volume_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volume of water used specifically for filter backwash operations during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). A sub-component of plant operations water. Tracked separately for filter performance monitoring and optimization of backwash frequency and duration.']
  chemical_dosing_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any unplanned or emergency chemical dosing event occurred during this production period (True = yes, False = no). Flags records requiring additional review in LIMS or SCADA systems. Supports operational exception reporting and chemical usage variance analysis.']
  cl2_residual_avg_mg_l decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average free chlorine residual concentration in finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Must meet the minimum residual disinfectant level of 0.2 mg/L at the point of entry to the distribution system. Critical for disinfection compliance and MOR reporting.']
  cl2_residual_min_mg_l decimal(6,3) [note: 'Minimum free chlorine residual concentration recorded in finished water during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Tracked to identify potential disinfection deficiency events. Any reading below 0.2 mg/L triggers regulatory notification requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production record was first created in the system (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality flag indicating the reliability of the production data in this record. \'GOOD\' indicates validated sensor/meter data; \'SUSPECT\' indicates data that may be erroneous pending investigation; \'ESTIMATED\' indicates values estimated due to meter failure or outage; \'MISSING\' indicates data gaps; \'CORRECTED\' indicates values that have been manually corrected from original readings. Aligns with OSIsoft PI Historian data quality coding conventions.']
  design_capacity_mgd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Permitted design treatment capacity of the WTP for this production period, measured in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). Captured at the record level to support capacity utilization calculations even if the plant\'s permitted capacity changes over time. Used for regulatory capacity compliance reporting.']
  disinfection_method varchar [note: 'Primary disinfection technology used during this production period. \'CHLORINATION\' uses free chlorine; \'UV\' uses Ultraviolet (UV) disinfection; \'CHLORAMINATION\' uses combined chlorine (chloramines); \'OZONATION\' uses ozone; \'COMBINED\' uses multiple methods. Determines applicable CT requirements and DBP formation potential.']
  filter_to_waste_volume_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volume of water discharged to waste during filter ripening (filter-to-waste) operations following backwash, measured in Million Gallons (MG). A sub-component of plant operations water. Tracked for regulatory compliance with turbidity control requirements and water loss accounting.']
  finished_water_volume_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volume of finished (treated) water produced by the WTP during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). This is the primary production output metric used in MOR reporting, water balance calculations, and capacity utilization analysis. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian clearwell/effluent flow meters.']
  fluoride_avg_mg_l decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average fluoride concentration in finished water during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). The EPA Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for fluoride is 4.0 mg/L; the secondary standard is 2.0 mg/L. The optimal fluoridation level recommended by HHS is 0.7 mg/L. Reported in MOR and Consumer Confidence Report (CCR).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or supervisor notes related to this production record. Used to document unusual operating conditions, equipment issues, source water quality events, regulatory exceedances, or other contextual information relevant to MOR reporting and operational review.']
  peak_production_rate_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum instantaneous finished water production flow rate recorded during this production period, measured in Gallons per Minute (GPM). Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian. Used for peak demand analysis, pump sizing validation, and hydraulic capacity planning.']
  ph_avg decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period. Optimal range is typically 7.0–8.5 for corrosion control compliance under the Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR). Critical for disinfection effectiveness (CT compliance) and distribution system corrosion control.']
  plant_efficiency_ratio decimal(6,4) [note: 'Ratio of finished water volume produced to source water volume intake, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9250 = 92.50% efficiency). Calculated as finished_water_volume_mg / source_water_volume_mg. A key operational Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for WTP performance benchmarking and water loss identification. Stored as a raw ratio to support analytics without recalculation.']
  plant_ops_water_volume_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volume of finished water consumed for internal plant operations during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). Includes filter backwash water, filter-to-waste volumes, and other in-plant process uses. Used in water balance accounting and plant efficiency calculations.']
  production_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the finished water production occurred (yyyy-MM-dd). The principal real-world event date for this production record. Used as the primary time dimension for MOR reporting, water balance calculations, and daily production trend analysis. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  production_record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this production record, used in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) and regulatory submissions. Format: WTP-PRD-YYYY-MM-DD-SHIFT. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  production_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the production record workflow. \'draft\' indicates data entry in progress; \'submitted\' indicates sent for supervisor review; \'approved\' indicates validated and accepted for MOR reporting; \'rejected\' indicates returned for correction; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a corrected record. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  regulatory_exceedance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any water quality parameter exceeded its Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) or treatment technique requirement during this production period (True = exceedance occurred, False = all parameters within limits). Triggers mandatory regulatory notification and public notice requirements under SDWA.']
  scada_data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the primary data source for the volumetric and quality measurements in this production record. \'PI_HISTORIAN\' indicates automated ingestion from OSIsoft PI Historian; \'MANUAL_ENTRY\' indicates operator-entered data; \'SCADA_DCS\' indicates direct SCADA/DCS system feed; \'AMI\' indicates Advanced Metering Infrastructure; \'HYBRID\' indicates a combination of automated and manual sources. Supports data lineage and quality confidence scoring.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Operational shift during which this production record applies. \'DAY\' typically covers 06:00–14:00, \'EVENING\' covers 14:00–22:00, \'NIGHT\' covers 22:00–06:00, and \'FULL_DAY\' represents a consolidated 24-hour daily record. Used for shift-level production analysis and operator accountability.']
  shift_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the production shift ended (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to precisely bound the production measurement window for SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian data integration and shift-level volume reconciliation.']
  shift_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the production shift began (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to precisely bound the production measurement window for SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian data integration and shift-level volume reconciliation.']
  source_water_volume_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volume of raw source water drawn into the WTP during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). Used as the input denominator for plant efficiency ratio calculations and water balance accounting. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian intake flow meters.']
  temperature_avg_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average temperature of finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period, measured in degrees Celsius (°C). Temperature affects disinfection Contact Time (CT) calculations, chlorine decay rates, and distribution system water quality. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian.']
  toc_avg_mg_l decimal(8,3) [note: 'Average Total Organic Carbon (TOC) concentration in finished water during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). TOC is a precursor to Disinfection Byproducts (DBPs) including Trihalomethanes (THMs) and Haloacetic Acids (HAA5). Monitored for Stage 2 Disinfectants and Disinfection Byproducts Rule (DBPR) compliance.']
  treatment_process_type varchar [note: 'Primary treatment process technology employed at the WTP for this production record. \'CONVENTIONAL\' includes coagulation/flocculation/sedimentation/filtration; \'DIRECT_FILTRATION\' omits sedimentation; \'MEMBRANE\' includes Reverse Osmosis (RO), Ultrafiltration (UF), or Microfiltration (MF); \'SOFTENING\' includes lime softening; \'GROUNDWATER\' includes well-based supply with disinfection only.']
  turbidity_avg_ntu decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average turbidity of finished water measured at the WTP effluent during this production period, in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Must comply with the Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) maximum of 0.3 NTU in 95% of monthly samples. Primary finished water quality summary metric for MOR reporting.']
  turbidity_max_ntu decimal(8,4) [note: 'Maximum turbidity value recorded in finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period, in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). The regulatory Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for turbidity is 1 NTU. Tracked separately from average to identify peak exceedance events for regulatory reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production record was last modified (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Tracks data corrections and supervisor approvals.']
  volume_pumped_to_distribution_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volume of finished water pumped from the WTP clearwell into the distribution network during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). Distinct from finished water produced — the difference represents storage changes. Critical for water balance and Non-Revenue Water (NRW) calculations.']

  Note: 'Daily and shift-level finished water production records for each WTP. Captures production date, shift, total volume produced (MG), volume pumped to distribution (MG), volume used for plant operations (backwash, filter-to-waste), source water volume intake (MG), plant efficiency ratio, and finished water quality summary (turbidity, chlorine residual, pH, fluoride). Primary record for MOR production reporting and water balance calculations.'
}

Table treatment.source_water_intake {
  source_water_intake_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each source water intake event record in the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) operations system. Primary key for this entity.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Intake structures are delivered via capital projects. Required for asset capitalization, linking intake permits to construction permits, tracking intake improvements, and connecting as-built drawings ']
  construction_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_contract. Business justification: Source water intake structures are built or rehabilitated under construction contracts. Warranty claims, defect liability periods, and contractor performance tracking require linking each intake asset']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Raw water acquisition and intake operations (pumping energy, screening, raw water purchase agreements) are discrete O&M budget line items. Budget officers track actual intake volumes and costs against']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant. Business justification: EPA Drinking Water SRF and watershed protection grants are tied to specific intake locations and source water protection programs. Grant administrators and auditors require the intake-to-grant link to']
  intake_meter_installation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.installation. Business justification: Source water intake events reference the meter installation at the intake point measuring raw water withdrawal volumes. Withdrawal permits require metered intake volumes for compliance tracking. Water']
  intake_pump_station_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the pump station asset through which raw water was drawn from the source. Links to the asset/facility master record managed in IBM Maximo CMMS.']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.process_unit. Business justification: Raw water intake events feed into specific treatment process units (e.g., raw water screening, pre-sedimentation, raw water storage). source_water_intake currently links to facility but not to the spe']
  raw_water_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Source water intake events require raw water quality samples (turbidity, TOC, pH, algae) for source water assessment reporting and treatment optimization decisions. Role prefix \'raw_water_\' distinguis']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Intake structures (screens, gates, pumps) are registered assets. Linking intake events to the asset registry enables maintenance scheduling, condition assessment correlation, and capital planning for ']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Source water intake monitoring is mandated by specific regulations (source water assessment rules, withdrawal permit requirements). source_water_intake has permit_compliance_status as a denormalized p']
  source_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) that received the raw water intake. Links to the facility master record.']
  source_wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) that received the raw water intake. Links to the facility master record.']
  storage_tank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.storage_tank. Business justification: In some configurations, raw water intake pumps directly to distribution storage tanks (common in smaller systems or emergency interconnections). Operators track which tank received raw water for blend']
  treatment_permit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the regulatory water withdrawal permit under which this intake event was authorized. Links to the permit master record for compliance tracking.']
  water_source_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the specific raw water source body or groundwater well from which water was withdrawn. Links to the water source master record (e.g., river, reservoir, aquifer, well).']
  algae_count_cells_per_ml decimal(14,2) [note: 'Algae cell count in raw water at intake, measured in cells per milliliter (cells/mL). Elevated algae counts indicate potential for taste and odor issues, cyanotoxin presence, and increased treatment chemical demand. Triggers source water protection alerts and treatment process modifications.']
  conductivity_us_per_cm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Electrical conductivity of raw water at intake, measured in microsiemens per centimeter (µS/cm). Serves as a proxy indicator for Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) concentration and overall ionic strength of the source water. Used to assess suitability for treatment processes such as Reverse Osmosis (RO).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this source water intake record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality assurance flag indicating the reliability and validation status of the intake event data. \'validated\' indicates data has passed QA/QC review; \'provisional\' indicates data is pending final validation; \'suspect\' indicates potential measurement anomaly requiring investigation; \'invalid\' indicates data has been rejected; \'estimated\' indicates values were estimated.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or method by which the intake event data was captured. \'scada_pi_historian\' indicates automated capture from OSIsoft PI Historian; \'manual_entry\' indicates operator-entered data; \'lims\' indicates data sourced from the Laboratory Information Management System; \'estimated\' indicates values were estimated due to meter failure.']
  dissolved_oxygen_mg_per_l decimal(6,3) [note: 'Dissolved oxygen concentration in raw water at intake, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Indicates biological health of the source water body and potential for anaerobic conditions that can release iron, manganese, and hydrogen sulfide, affecting treatment requirements.']
  flow_rate_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average flow rate of raw water intake during the event, expressed in Gallons Per Minute (GPM). Captured from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian flow meter readings at the intake pump station. Used for hydraulic modeling and pump performance analysis.']
  gis_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the raw water intake point in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for spatial analysis, GIS mapping of intake locations, and source water protection zone delineation in Esri ArcGIS.']
  gis_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the raw water intake point in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for spatial analysis, GIS mapping of intake locations, and source water protection zone delineation in Esri ArcGIS.']
  intake_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the raw water intake event concluded. Used with intake_timestamp to calculate duration and total volume withdrawn during the event.']
  intake_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the intake event, used in operational logs, regulatory submissions, and Monthly Operating Reports (MOR). Format: SWI-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  intake_method varchar [note: 'Method by which raw water was conveyed from the source to the treatment facility. \'gravity\' indicates flow by gravity through an intake channel or conduit; \'pumped\' indicates mechanical pumping; \'combined\' indicates both methods; \'emergency_bypass\' indicates use of an emergency intake route.']
  intake_screen_condition varchar [note: 'Operational condition of the intake screening equipment (bar screens, traveling screens) at the time of the intake event. Blocked or bypassed screens affect flow rates and may allow debris into the treatment process, impacting downstream equipment and water quality.']
  intake_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the intake event record. \'active\' indicates intake is in progress; \'completed\' indicates the event concluded normally; \'suspended\' indicates intake was halted due to quality or operational issue; \'cancelled\' indicates the event was voided; \'pending_review\' indicates the record requires quality assurance review.']
  intake_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world date and time at which the raw water intake event commenced, as recorded by the SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian system at the intake pump station. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  mor_reporting_period varchar [note: 'The regulatory Monthly Operating Report (MOR) reporting period (YYYY-MM) to which this intake event belongs. Used to aggregate intake volumes and quality data for submission to State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes recorded by the plant operator regarding the intake event, including any anomalies, equipment issues, unusual source water conditions, or actions taken. Supports operational log requirements and regulatory audit trails.']
  number_of_pumps_active varchar [note: 'Count of intake pumps operating simultaneously during the intake event. Used for energy consumption analysis, pump performance benchmarking, and maintenance scheduling in IBM Maximo CMMS.']
  permit_daily_limit_mg decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum daily volume of raw water authorized for withdrawal under the applicable water withdrawal permit, expressed in Million Gallons (MG). Used to assess compliance status against actual volume withdrawn.']
  ph_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of raw water at the point of intake, measured on the standard 0-14 scale. Influences coagulation efficiency, disinfection effectiveness, and corrosion control treatment. Monitored per Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR) requirements.']
  pump_station_operating_pressure_psi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Operating pressure at the intake pump station during the intake event, measured in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI). Monitored via SCADA to ensure pumps operate within design parameters and to detect potential main breaks or pressure anomalies.']
  raw_water_color_cpu decimal(8,2) [note: 'Apparent color of raw water at intake, measured in Color Units (CPU) using the platinum-cobalt standard. High color values indicate elevated natural organic matter (NOM) and humic substances, which are precursors to Disinfection Byproducts (DBP) including Trihalomethanes (THM) and Haloacetic Acids (HAA5).']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the raw water source type. Determines applicable treatment processes, regulatory monitoring requirements, and source water assessment protocols under SDWA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: surface_water|groundwater|purchased_water|recycled_water|brackish_water|other — promote to reference product]']
  source_water_alert_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an active source water quality alert or advisory was in effect at the time of this intake event (e.g., algal bloom advisory, upstream contamination notice, drought restriction). When True, triggers enhanced monitoring protocols and treatment process review.']
  source_water_protection_zone varchar [note: 'Designation of the source water protection zone or wellhead protection area associated with this intake point, as defined in the utility\'s Source Water Assessment and Protection (SWAP) program. Used for land use management and contamination risk assessment.']
  tds_mg_per_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) concentration of raw water at intake, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Indicates overall mineral content and salinity of the source water. Drives decisions on treatment technology selection including Reverse Osmosis (RO) and ion exchange.']
  temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature of raw water at the point of intake, measured in degrees Celsius. Affects disinfection Contact Time (CT) calculations, chemical dosing rates, biological activity, and seasonal treatment process adjustments.']
  toc_mg_per_l decimal(8,3) [note: 'Total Organic Carbon (TOC) concentration of raw water at intake, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Critical parameter for predicting Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) formation potential including Trihalomethanes (THM) and Haloacetic Acids (HAA5). Drives treatment process adjustments.']
  turbidity_ntu decimal(8,3) [note: 'Turbidity of raw water at the point of intake, measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). A key raw water quality parameter that drives coagulation chemical dosing decisions and treatment process optimization. Captured at intake via inline turbidimeter or LIMS sample.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this source water intake record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Tracks data corrections, QA/QC updates, and regulatory review amendments. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  upstream_event_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any known upstream events or conditions that may affect raw water quality at the time of intake (e.g., upstream spill, agricultural runoff, upstream WWTP discharge, construction activity). Supports source water protection program documentation.']
  volume_withdrawn_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volume of raw water withdrawn during the intake event, expressed in Million Gallons (MG). This is the principal quantitative measurement for the intake event, used for permit compliance, production planning, and Non-Revenue Water (NRW) calculations.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition at the time of the intake event. Weather conditions significantly influence raw water quality parameters (turbidity spikes during storm events, algae blooms during drought/heat). Used for source water quality trend analysis and treatment process correlation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|heavy_rain|storm|snow|drought|flood_event — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records raw water intake events from surface water or groundwater sources. Captures intake date/time, source water body or well ID, volume withdrawn (MG), raw water quality parameters at intake (turbidity NTU, TOC mg/L, temperature, pH, conductivity, algae count), intake pump station ID, withdrawal permit limit, and compliance status. Supports source water protection program and intake optimization.'
}

Table treatment.chemical_inventory {
  chemical_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each chemical inventory record in the water treatment facility. Primary key for the chemical_inventory data product.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Chemical inventory replenishment is directly triggered by AP invoices from chemical suppliers. The three-way match process (PO → chemical receipt → AP invoice) requires linking inventory records to th']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the water treatment or wastewater treatment plant (WTP/WWTP) where this chemical inventory record is maintained. Links to the facility master record.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Chemical inventory is a balance sheet asset (unit_cost and quantity_on_hand present). Required for inventory valuation, COGS calculation, financial statement preparation, and GL posting to inventory a']
  inventory_stock_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_stock. Business justification: Water utilities must reconcile treatment-domain chemical inventory (with regulatory attributes: EPCRA, RMP, Tier II) against supply-domain warehouse stock records. This link enables chemical stock rec']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Treatment chemicals (chlorine, alum, polymer, caustic) are materials with ERP material numbers, procurement specifications, and inventory management requirements. Fundamental link between treatment ch']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.process_unit. Business justification: chemical_inventory has a dosing_point STRING column identifying where the chemical is applied. This is a free-text reference to a treatment process unit (e.g., \'Pre-chlorination\', \'Post-filtration\'). ']
  storage_asset_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Chemical inventory management requires precise physical location tracking within facilities for safety compliance (EPCRA Tier II reporting), emergency response planning, and operational logistics. Sto']
  storage_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Chemical storage tanks and containment structures are registered assets requiring condition assessment, RMP/PSM secondary containment inspections, and maintenance scheduling. Role-prefixed \'storage_as']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.vendor']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage tank, container, or designated storage area within the facility where the chemical is stored. Used for precise physical location tracking and OSHA Tier II reporting.']
  average_daily_usage decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average quantity of the chemical consumed per day at the facility, expressed in the designated unit of measure. Calculated from historical dosing records and used to estimate days of supply remaining and optimize reorder frequency.']
  avg_quantity_in_year decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average daily quantity of the chemical present at the facility during the Tier II reporting year, expressed in the designated unit of measure. Required field for OSHA EPCRA Section 312 Tier II annual report submission.']
  chemical_code varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.chemical. Business justification: Chemical inventory should reference the chemical master table for authoritative chemical properties (NSF certification, regulatory status, dose limits, storage requirements). The chemical_inventory ta']
  container_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or tag number assigned to the specific storage tank or container holding this chemical (e.g., T-101, DRUM-045). Used for physical asset tracking, maintenance records in IBM Maximo, and OSHA Tier II site maps.']
  days_of_supply decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated number of days the current quantity on hand will last based on average daily usage. Provides an operational indicator for procurement urgency and treatment process continuity risk management.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Manufacturer-specified expiration or shelf-life date for the chemical lot. Chemicals used beyond expiration may degrade in efficacy, impacting treatment performance and regulatory compliance. Triggers disposal or replacement workflows.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT hazardous materials classification code for the chemical (e.g., Class 8 Corrosive, Class 2.3 Toxic Gas, Class 5.1 Oxidizer). Required for transportation compliance, emergency response planning, and OSHA Tier II reporting.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the chemical inventory record indicating whether the chemical is actively in use, on order, depleted, quarantined due to quality concerns, expired, or discontinued from use at the facility.']
  is_epcra_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this chemical meets the threshold quantity for mandatory reporting under EPCRA Section 312 (Tier II) to state and local emergency planning committees. True if the chemical is an Extremely Hazardous Substance (EHS) or meets reporting thresholds.']
  is_rmp_regulated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this chemical is a regulated substance under EPA Risk Management Program (RMP) rules (40 CFR Part 68). True if the chemical is listed and stored above the threshold quantity, requiring an RMP plan submission.']
  last_delivery_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent chemical delivery received at the facility. Used to track supply chain cadence, validate inventory levels, and support procurement planning.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count or verification performed at the storage location. Required for inventory accuracy audits and OSHA Tier II annual reporting reconciliation.']
  last_physical_count_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity recorded during the most recent physical inventory count, expressed in the designated unit of measure. Used to reconcile system quantity against physical stock and identify discrepancies.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or supplier-assigned lot or batch number for the chemical delivery. Enables traceability for quality incidents, product recalls, and treatment process investigations.']
  max_quantity_in_year decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum quantity of the chemical present at the facility at any point during the Tier II reporting year, expressed in the designated unit of measure. Required field for OSHA EPCRA Section 312 Tier II annual report submission.']
  max_storage_capacity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable quantity of the chemical that can be stored at the facility location, expressed in the designated unit of measure. Governed by OSHA PSM thresholds, EPA RMP requirements, and local fire codes. Used for OSHA Tier II reporting.']
  maximo_item_number varchar [note: 'Item number assigned to this chemical in IBM Maximo Asset Management (CMMS) storeroom inventory. Enables cross-referencing between the lakehouse chemical inventory and the operational CMMS for work order material consumption tracking.']
  nfpa_fire_rating varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 flammability hazard rating for the chemical on a scale of 0 (will not burn) to 4 (extremely flammable). Used for fire safety planning, storage segregation requirements, and emergency response.']
  nfpa_health_rating varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 health hazard rating for the chemical on a scale of 0 (minimal hazard) to 4 (deadly). Used for emergency response planning, worker safety training, and facility hazard communication.']
  nfpa_reactivity_rating varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 instability/reactivity hazard rating for the chemical on a scale of 0 (stable) to 4 (may detonate). Critical for chemical storage segregation, incompatibility management, and emergency response planning.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP purchase order number associated with the most recent or active procurement of this chemical. Supports procurement traceability and accounts payable reconciliation.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current physical quantity of the chemical available in storage at the facility, expressed in the designated unit of measure (lbs or gallons). Updated after each delivery receipt and physical count. Critical for treatment process continuity and procurement planning.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this chemical inventory record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this chemical inventory record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum quantity threshold at which a replenishment purchase order should be triggered to prevent stockout and ensure uninterrupted treatment operations. Expressed in the same unit of measure as quantity_on_hand.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Buffer quantity of chemical maintained above the reorder point to protect against supply chain disruptions, delivery delays, or unexpected demand spikes. Expressed in the same unit of measure as quantity_on_hand.']
  sap_material_number varchar [note: 'SAP ERP material master number assigned to this chemical in the MM module. Enables traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and SAP procurement, goods receipt, and inventory management transactions.']
  sds_reference_number varchar [note: 'Document reference number or identifier for the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with this chemical, as required by OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom). Links to the SDS document management system for emergency response and worker safety.']
  sds_revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most current revision of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) on file for this chemical. OSHA requires SDS documents to be current; outdated SDS may indicate a compliance gap.']
  storage_container_type varchar [note: 'Type of storage vessel or container used to hold the chemical at the facility (e.g., bulk tank, day tank, drum, cylinder, tote, bag). Relevant for spill response planning, OSHA Tier II, and maintenance scheduling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bulk_tank|day_tank|drum|cylinder|tote|bag|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature for the chemical in degrees Celsius, as specified in the SDS. Exceeding this threshold may cause chemical degradation, hazardous reactions, or loss of treatment efficacy.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature for the chemical in degrees Celsius, as specified in the SDS. Ensures chemical integrity and prevents hazardous reactions due to improper storage conditions.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-assigned part or catalog number for this chemical product as used in purchase orders and delivery documentation. Facilitates accurate procurement and receiving.']
  tier2_reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which this chemical inventory record is applicable for OSHA EPCRA Section 312 Tier II annual hazardous chemical inventory reporting. Supports annual regulatory submission to state and local emergency planning committees.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of measure for the chemical as established in the most recent purchase order or contract. Used for chemical cost tracking, OPEX budgeting, and treatment cost-per-gallon calculations. Expressed in USD.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure used to quantify the chemical inventory (e.g., lbs for dry chemicals, gallons for liquid chemicals). Must be consistent with procurement orders and dosing calculations.']

  Note: 'Tracks on-site chemical inventory levels at each treatment facility. Captures chemical name, CAS number, storage tank or container ID, current quantity on hand (lbs/gallons), unit of measure, reorder point, maximum storage capacity, last delivery date, last physical count date, and safety data sheet (SDS) reference. Supports chemical procurement planning, OSHA Tier II reporting, and treatment process continuity.'
}

Table treatment.process_control_setpoint {
  process_control_setpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each process control setpoint record in the treatment domain. Primary key for the process_control_setpoint data product.']
  chemical_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.chemical. Business justification: Process control setpoints for chemical dosing (chlorine residual target, coagulant dose rate, pH adjustment) are specific to a particular chemical. process_control_setpoint has parameter_type (STRING)']
  contaminant_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.contaminant_limit. Business justification: Operators set process control setpoints (chlorine residual targets, turbidity alarm thresholds) based on regulatory contaminant limits. Links operational setpoint to regulatory driver for compliance-d']
  pi_point_id varchar [note: 'The unique PI Point identifier in the OSIsoft PI Historian system corresponding to this setpoint\'s SCADA tag. Used for direct data retrieval and synchronization between the Silver Layer setpoint register and the PI Historian real-time data store.']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.pm_schedule. Business justification: Setpoint adjustments frequently follow preventive maintenance activities (instrument calibration, control loop tuning, sensor replacement). Water treatment operators need to track which PM events trig']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: Treatment process control setpoints (chlorine residual targets, flow rate limits) are zone-specific — operators configure different target residuals for each pressure zone served. Regulatory complianc']
  previous_setpoint_process_control_setpoint_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding version of this setpoint record (self-referential). Enables reconstruction of the full setpoint change history chain for regulatory audit and process optimization analysis.']
  primary_process_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the treatment facility (WTP or WWTP) where this setpoint is configured and enforced. Links the setpoint to its operational site.']
  primary_process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment process unit (e.g., filter unit, clarifier, chlorination contact chamber, RO train, UV reactor) to which this setpoint applies.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Control setpoints are configured for specific physical instruments and assets (chlorine analyzers, flow meters). Linking setpoints to the asset registry enables change management audits, calibration t']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Process control setpoints (chlorine residual alarms, turbidity trip limits) are established to ensure compliance with specific MCLs and treatment technique requirements. process_control_setpoint has r']
  treatment_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.treatment_permit. Business justification: Process control setpoints are governed by regulatory permit limits (e.g., turbidity limit, chlorine residual limit). The permit_number string on process_control_setpoint should be replaced by FK to th']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IBM Maximo work order that authorized or triggered this setpoint change (e.g., a preventive maintenance work order for filter media replacement that requires a setpoint adjustment). Null if the setpoint change was not associated with a maintenance work order.']
  alarm_delay_seconds varchar [note: 'The time delay in seconds before an alarm is activated after the process variable crosses an alarm limit. Prevents nuisance alarms from transient spikes. A value of 0 means immediate alarm activation.']
  alarm_priority varchar [note: 'The priority classification of alarms generated when this setpoint\'s limits are breached. \'critical\' alarms require immediate operator response and may trigger automatic shutdown; \'high\' requires prompt response; \'medium\' and \'low\' are advisory. Aligns with ISA-18.2 alarm management philosophy.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the setpoint was formally approved by the certified operator (referenced by operator_id). Required for regulatory audit trails demonstrating that setpoint changes were authorized by a qualified operator.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the business or operational reason for creating or modifying this setpoint record (e.g., \'Seasonal chloramine conversion\', \'Post-LCRR compliance adjustment\', \'Filter media replacement\', \'Regulatory agency directive\'). Required for change management audit trails.']
  control_mode varchar [note: 'The control mode under which this setpoint operates in the SCADA/PLC system. \'automatic\' means the control loop maintains the setpoint autonomously; \'manual\' means operator-directed; \'cascade\' means the output of one controller drives the setpoint of another; \'ratio\' and \'feedforward\' are advanced control strategies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this setpoint record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the setpoint lifecycle.']
  deadband_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The deadband or hysteresis value around the setpoint within which no corrective control action is taken. Prevents excessive actuator cycling (e.g., chemical dosing pump hunting). Expressed in the same engineering unit as setpoint_value.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this setpoint value became or becomes operationally effective in the control system. Used for temporal tracking of setpoint changes and regulatory audit trails. Aligns with MOR reporting periods.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'The engineering unit associated with the setpoint value and alarm limits (e.g., mg/L for chlorine residual, NTU for turbidity, pH for potential of hydrogen, GPM for flow rate, PSI for pressure). Critical for correct interpretation of all numeric fields on this record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg/L|NTU|pH|GPM|MGD|PSI|°C|°F|%|hours|minutes|mg/L/min|mV|ft|scfm — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this setpoint version expires or is scheduled to be superseded. Null for open-ended setpoints with no planned expiry. Supports scheduled setpoint reviews and seasonal adjustments (e.g., summer/winter chloramine dosing targets).']
  high_alarm_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The high alarm threshold for the process variable. When the measured value exceeds this limit, a high alarm is triggered in the SCADA/HMI system, alerting operators to take corrective action. Expressed in the same engineering unit as setpoint_value.']
  high_trip_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The high-high (HH) trip or shutdown limit for the process variable. Exceeding this value triggers an automatic safety interlock or process shutdown (e.g., chlorine over-feed trip, filter bypass). More severe than the high alarm limit. Expressed in the same engineering unit as setpoint_value.']
  is_ct_compliance_parameter boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this setpoint is a CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) compliance parameter as required under the EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule. CT compliance parameters (e.g., chlorine residual, contact time) are subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny and must be tracked in MOR submissions.']
  is_mcl_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this setpoint is directly associated with an EPA or state MCL parameter (e.g., chlorine residual, turbidity, pH for corrosion control under LCRR). MCL-related setpoints require additional documentation and regulatory reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this setpoint record was most recently updated. Tracks the most recent change event for audit and change management purposes.']
  low_alarm_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The low alarm threshold for the process variable. When the measured value falls below this limit, a low alarm is triggered in the SCADA/HMI system. Expressed in the same engineering unit as setpoint_value.']
  low_trip_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The low-low (LL) trip or shutdown limit for the process variable. Falling below this value triggers an automatic safety interlock or process shutdown (e.g., chlorine under-feed trip, pump cavitation protection). More severe than the low alarm limit. Expressed in the same engineering unit as setpoint_value.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational context, special conditions, or instructions associated with this setpoint (e.g., \'Applies only during peak demand hours\', \'Coordinate with distribution operations before adjustment\', \'Seasonal adjustment per SWTR CT table\'). Supplements the change_reason field.']
  operator_certification_grade varchar [note: 'The certification grade level of the operator who approved this setpoint (e.g., Grade I through Grade IV per state drinking water operator certification programs). Ensures the approving operator holds the appropriate grade for the facility classification.']
  operator_license_number varchar [note: 'The state-issued operator certification license number of the operator who approved this setpoint. Stored directly on the setpoint record for regulatory traceability without requiring a join, as required by MOR and DMR submissions.']
  parameter_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the process variable being controlled (e.g., water_quality for chlorine/pH/turbidity, flow_rate for GPM targets, pressure for PSI limits, chemical_dose for mg/L targets, equipment_runtime for filter run time). Drives analytics segmentation and alarm management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: water_quality|flow_rate|pressure|chemical_dose|equipment_runtime|level|temperature — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  process_area varchar [note: 'The treatment process area or stage to which this setpoint belongs. Classifies the setpoint within the treatment train for process control, analytics, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coagulation_flocculation|sedimentation|filtration|disinfection|chemical_dosing|membrane_treatment|residuals_management|distribution_pumping|wastewater_primary|wastewater_secondary|wastewater_tertiary — promote to reference product]']
  ramp_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum rate at which the setpoint value may be changed per unit time (expressed in engineering_unit per minute). Prevents sudden process upsets during setpoint transitions (e.g., gradual chlorine dose ramp-up after a plant restart). Null if no ramp rate constraint applies.']
  ramp_rate_unit varchar [note: 'The time unit denominator for the ramp_rate field (e.g., per_minute, per_hour). Clarifies the rate of change constraint for the setpoint transition.']
  scada_tag varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian or SCADA/PLC tag name that uniquely identifies the process variable associated with this setpoint (e.g., WTP01.CL2.RESIDUAL.SP, WTP01.FILTER1.TURBIDITY.HH). Used for real-time integration between the setpoint register and the SCADA/PI Historian system.']
  setpoint_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the setpoint parameter type within the facility\'s process control register (e.g., CL2_RESIDUAL_SP, PH_TARGET_SP, TURB_ALARM_HI). Used for cross-system integration and MOR regulatory submissions.']
  setpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the process control setpoint parameter (e.g., \'Chlorine Residual Target\', \'pH Target\', \'Turbidity Alarm Threshold\', \'Filter Run Time Limit\', \'Chemical Dose Rate\'). Serves as the primary identity label for the setpoint in operator interfaces and regulatory reports.']
  setpoint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the setpoint record. \'active\' indicates the setpoint is currently enforced in the control system. \'pending_approval\' indicates it has been proposed but not yet approved by a certified operator. \'superseded\' indicates it has been replaced by a newer version. \'under_review\' indicates it is being evaluated for change.']
  setpoint_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The target or nominal value for the process control parameter. This is the principal quantitative fact — the value the control system actively maintains (e.g., 1.5 for chlorine residual in mg/L, 7.2 for pH, 0.1 for turbidity in NTU). Corresponds to the SP (setpoint) field in OSIsoft PI Historian.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this setpoint was originated or last synchronized (e.g., OSIsoft_PI for PI Historian-sourced setpoints, SCADA_PLC for direct PLC configuration, Maximo for maintenance-driven setpoints, Manual_Entry for operator-entered values).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this setpoint record, incremented each time the setpoint value or limits are modified. Supports full version history and audit trail for regulatory compliance. Version 1 is the initial approved setpoint.']

  Note: 'Master configuration of process control setpoints for treatment process units. Captures setpoint parameter name (chlorine residual target, pH target, turbidity alarm threshold, filter run time limit, chemical dose rate), setpoint value, engineering unit, high/low alarm limits, high/high and low/low trip limits, effective date, approved-by operator, and associated SCADA tag. Provides the authoritative setpoint register for process control and regulatory compliance.'
}

Table treatment.treatment_permit {
  treatment_permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the treatment operating permit record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the treatment_permit data product.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the treatment facility (Water Treatment Plant or Wastewater Treatment Plant) to which this permit is issued. Links the permit to its regulated facility.']
  regulatory_agency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory agency that issued this permit (e.g., EPA Region, State Primacy Agency, State Environmental Department). Used for compliance tracking and correspondence routing.']
  biosolids_permit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this permit includes authorization for biosolids or residuals management (land application, incineration, or landfill disposal). Relevant for WWTPs subject to EPA 40 CFR Part 503 biosolids regulations.']
  bod_limit_mg_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Permitted maximum concentration of Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) in treated effluent, expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). A primary indicator of organic pollution load in wastewater discharge. Used for DMR compliance evaluation.']
  chlorine_residual_limit_mg_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum or maximum permitted chlorine residual concentration in finished water or treated effluent, expressed in mg/L. For drinking water, a minimum residual is required at the point of entry to the distribution system. For NPDES discharge, a maximum may be set to protect aquatic life.']
  compliance_schedule_end_date date [note: 'The final date by which the facility must achieve full compliance with all permit limits per the regulatory-approved compliance schedule. Null if no compliance schedule is in effect. Used to track CIP project milestones and regulatory commitments.']
  compliance_schedule_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit includes a formal compliance schedule (a regulatory-approved timeline for achieving permit limits that the facility cannot currently meet). True triggers tracking of interim milestones and final compliance dates in the compliance management system.']
  ct_requirement_mg_min_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum required CT (Contact Time) value for disinfection as specified in the permit, expressed in mg·min/L (product of disinfectant concentration and contact time). CT compliance is a core Surface Water Treatment Rule requirement for Giardia and virus inactivation credit.']
  dmr_submission_method varchar [note: 'The required or preferred method for submitting Discharge Monitoring Reports (DMRs) or Monthly Operating Reports (MORs) to the regulatory agency. NetDMR is the EPA\'s electronic DMR system; eDMR refers to state-specific electronic systems; paper and state_portal are alternatives.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Document management system reference, file path, or URL pointing to the official permit document, fact sheet, and associated attachments stored in the enterprise document repository. Enables direct access to the authoritative permit text for compliance review.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit conditions become legally binding and enforceable. May differ from the issue date if a grace period or administrative continuance applies.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit term expires. Triggers renewal workflow and compliance calendar alerts. Under administrative continuance provisions, operations may continue under the expired permit while renewal is pending.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the treatment facility covered by this permit. Water Treatment Plant (WTP) produces finished drinking water; Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) or Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) treats and discharges wastewater; pump_station may hold ancillary permits; combined covers co-located facilities.']
  haa5_mcl_ug_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for Haloacetic Acids (HAA5) as specified in the permit or applicable regulatory standard, expressed in micrograms per liter (µg/L). The EPA MCL for HAA5 is 60 µg/L. A key Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) compliance parameter for drinking water permits.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory agency formally issued or reissued the permit. Marks the start of the current permit term and is used to calculate permit age and renewal lead times.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory compliance inspection conducted by the issuing agency or primacy agency at the permitted facility. Used to track inspection frequency, prepare for upcoming inspections, and assess compliance standing.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of effluent or finished water quality monitoring as specified in the permit. Drives the sampling schedule in the LIMS and the reporting calendar for DMR/MOR submissions. Represents the most frequent monitoring requirement across all parameters.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Anticipated or scheduled date of the next regulatory compliance inspection. Supports proactive compliance preparation and internal audit scheduling. May be estimated based on inspection frequency requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, regulatory correspondence summaries, internal compliance observations, or context not captured in structured fields. Used by compliance staff to document permit history, agency communications, and action items.']
  npdes_major_minor_class varchar [note: 'EPA classification of the NPDES permit as major or minor based on facility size, discharge volume, and potential environmental impact. Major facilities are subject to more stringent oversight, mandatory DMR reporting, and EPA inspection priorities. Applicable only to NPDES permit types.']
  permit_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the permit is an individual permit (facility-specific terms and conditions), a general permit (covers a category of similar dischargers), or an emergency permit (temporary authorization). Determines the regulatory review process and compliance obligations.']
  permit_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the permit as assigned by the issuing agency or used internally (e.g., \'City Water Treatment Plant NPDES Discharge Permit\', \'Regional WTP Drinking Water Operating Permit\'). Supports human-readable identification in compliance registers and reports.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-assigned permit number as issued by the regulatory authority (e.g., NPDES permit number, state drinking water operating permit number). This is the official identifier used in all regulatory correspondence, DMRs, MORs, and CCRs.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the permit. Active indicates the permit is in force; pending_renewal indicates the renewal application has been submitted and the permit is administratively continued; expired indicates the permit term has lapsed without renewal; suspended or revoked indicates regulatory action has been taken.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the permit by regulatory program. NPDES governs wastewater discharge under the Clean Water Act; drinking_water_operating governs WTP operations under SDWA; air_emission covers process air releases; residuals_management covers biosolids/sludge disposal; industrial_pretreatment covers IUP programs; stormwater covers MS4 or industrial stormwater. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NPDES|drinking_water_operating|air_emission|residuals_management|industrial_pretreatment|stormwater|other — promote to reference product]']
  permitted_avg_flow_mgd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Monthly average flow rate authorized under this permit in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). Distinct from the peak daily limit; used for long-term capacity planning and average-basis compliance evaluation in DMR submissions.']
  permitted_flow_mgd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum daily flow rate authorized under this permit, expressed in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). This is the principal quantitative limit governing facility capacity and is used for capacity planning, compliance monitoring, and MOR/DMR reporting.']
  pfas_monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit requires monitoring for Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS) compounds. Reflects EPA\'s PFAS Action Plan and emerging state-level PFAS MCLs. Drives LIMS sampling plan configuration and CCR disclosure requirements.']
  ph_limit_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted pH value for treated effluent or finished water as specified in the permit. Used alongside ph_limit_min to define the acceptable pH range for compliance monitoring and corrosion control program evaluation.']
  ph_limit_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum permitted pH value for treated effluent or finished water as specified in the permit. pH is a key indicator of water chemistry and corrosion control. Typically 6.0–9.0 for NPDES effluent; 6.5–8.5 for drinking water under SDWA.']
  receiving_water_body varchar [note: 'Name of the surface water body (river, lake, estuary, or ocean) designated as the receiving water for permitted discharges (e.g., \'Ohio River\', \'Lake Michigan\'). Required for NPDES permits; used in water quality impact assessments and antidegradation analysis.']
  renewal_application_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit renewal application was submitted to the issuing regulatory agency. NPDES regulations typically require submission at least 180 days before expiration. Used to verify timely renewal and administrative continuance eligibility.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the permit renewal process. Tracks progression from initial application through agency review to final determination. Supports compliance calendar management and regulatory relationship tracking.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for submitting compliance reports (DMRs, MORs, or equivalent) to the regulatory agency as specified in the permit. Drives the compliance reporting calendar and submission deadlines tracked in the MOR submission workflow.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any special conditions, schedules of compliance, best management practices (BMPs), or additional requirements imposed by the regulatory agency beyond standard effluent limitations. Examples include PFAS monitoring requirements, CSO long-term control plan milestones, or LCRR service line inventory obligations.']
  term_years varchar [note: 'Duration of the permit in years as specified by the issuing agency. NPDES permits are typically issued for 5-year terms; state drinking water operating permits may vary. Used for renewal planning and compliance scheduling.']
  tss_limit_mg_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Permitted maximum concentration of Total Suspended Solids (TSS) in treated effluent, expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Core secondary treatment standard for WWTP NPDES compliance. Used in DMR reporting and effluent quality monitoring.']
  tthm_mcl_ug_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for Total Trihalomethanes (TTHMs) as specified in the permit or applicable regulatory standard, expressed in micrograms per liter (µg/L). The EPA MCL for TTHMs is 80 µg/L. A key Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) compliance parameter for drinking water permits.']
  turbidity_limit_ntu decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum permitted turbidity of finished water or treated effluent in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). For WTPs, the Surface Water Treatment Rule requires combined filter effluent turbidity not to exceed 0.3 NTU in 95% of measurements. Critical for MCL compliance.']
  violation_count_active varchar [note: 'Number of currently unresolved permit violations (effluent limit exceedances, reporting violations, or schedule of compliance violations) associated with this permit. Sourced from the regulatory agency\'s compliance tracking system. Used for compliance risk scoring and executive reporting.']

  Note: 'Master record of operating permits governing treatment facility operations. Captures permit type (NPDES, state drinking water operating permit, air permit, residuals permit), permit number, issuing regulatory agency, issue date, expiration date, permitted flow limits (MGD), effluent/finished water quality limits, special conditions, renewal status, and associated facility. Authoritative permit register for the treatment domain supporting compliance management.'
}

Table treatment.treatment_violation {
  treatment_violation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each treatment regulatory violation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  chemical_dose_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.chemical_dose_event. Business justification: Treatment violations (e.g., MCL exceedances, CT failures) are often caused by or directly linked to a specific chemical dosing event — under-dosing of disinfectant, incorrect chemical concentration, o']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Regulatory violations (MCL exceedances, CT failures) frequently trigger CIP projects for infrastructure upgrades. Utilities must demonstrate to regulators that a capital project was initiated in respo']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where the violation occurred.']
  filter_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.filter_run. Business justification: Turbidity MCL exceedances and filter performance violations are directly tied to specific filter runs. treatment_violation has process_area (STRING) but no FK to the specific filter_run that caused th']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Violations trigger penalty costs (penalty_amount field present), corrective action expenses, and legal fees. Required for penalty accrual, corrective action cost tracking, regulatory cost reporting, a']
  primary_treatment_analytical_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.analytical_result. Business justification: Treatment violations are triggered by specific analytical results exceeding regulatory limits. Linking treatment_violation directly to the triggering analytical_result provides the evidentiary basis f']
  process_reading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.process_alarm. Business justification: Treatment violations are often triggered by process alarms (e.g., CT compliance failure, turbidity exceedance). The scada_alarm_tag string on treatment_violation should be replaced by FK to the treatm']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.process_unit. Business justification: treatment_violation has process_area as a free-text STRING field identifying where the violation occurred. Normalizing this to process_unit_id provides a structured FK to the specific treatment proces']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Treatment violations are tied to specific assets (failed filter, malfunctioning UV reactor). Linking violations to the asset registry enables asset-level violation history for criticality rating updat']
  regulatory_agency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory agency (e.g., EPA, State Primacy Agency) to which this violation must be reported.']
  source_water_intake_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.source_water_intake. Business justification: Treatment violations can be triggered by source water quality events — high turbidity, algal blooms, or contamination events at the intake. source_water_intake captures these raw water quality paramet']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.territory. Business justification: SDWA Tier 1/2/3 public notification requirements mandate identifying the affected service territory when treatment violations occur. treatment_violation already has public_notification_required and pu']
  treatment_permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the NPDES permit under which the violation was recorded, applicable for wastewater discharge violations at WWTP facilities.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IBM Maximo CMMS work order created to execute the corrective action plan for this violation. Links compliance management to maintenance and operations workflows.']
  ccr_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation must be disclosed in the annual Consumer Confidence Report (CCR) sent to customers, as required by EPA SDWA regulations for community water systems.']
  contaminant_code varchar [note: 'EPA or state-assigned contaminant code for the parameter that was violated (e.g., TTHM for Total Trihalomethanes, PFAS, turbidity, lead, copper, BOD, TSS). Aligns with EPA SDWIS contaminant codes.']
  contaminant_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the contaminant or treatment parameter involved in the violation (e.g., Total Trihalomethanes, Turbidity, Lead, Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS), Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD)).']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the corrective action plan must be completed and compliance restored, as agreed with the regulatory agency or mandated by the compliance schedule.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the corrective action plan developed to address the root cause of the violation and restore compliance. Includes immediate remediation steps, process adjustments, and preventive measures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this violation record was first created in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the violation was detected, either through SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian alarm, laboratory result, or operator observation. Supports CT compliance and public notification deadline calculations.']
  dmr_reporting_period varchar [note: 'The DMR reporting period (YYYY-MM) in which this violation must be included for NPDES permit compliance reporting. Applicable for WWTP effluent violations. Links to the corresponding DMR submission.']
  enforcement_action_taken boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulatory agency has initiated a formal enforcement action (e.g., Notice of Violation, Administrative Order, Consent Order, penalty) in response to this violation.']
  enforcement_action_type varchar [note: 'Type of formal enforcement action taken by the regulatory agency (e.g., Notice of Violation (NOV), Administrative Order (AO), Consent Order, Civil Penalty, Criminal Referral). Null if no enforcement action was taken. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NOV|administrative_order|consent_order|civil_penalty|criminal_referral|compliance_schedule|none — promote to reference product]']
  exceedance_magnitude decimal(18,6) [note: 'The numeric difference between the measured value and the regulatory limit (measured_value minus regulatory_limit). Positive values indicate the degree of exceedance above the limit, supporting severity assessment and trend analysis.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Type of treatment facility where the violation occurred: WTP (Water Treatment Plant), WWTP (Wastewater Treatment Plant), STP (Sewage Treatment Plant), booster station, or storage facility.']
  is_repeat_violation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation is a repeat occurrence of the same contaminant or parameter violation at the same facility within the preceding 12-month period. Repeat violations may trigger escalated regulatory response and increased penalties.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual measured concentration, level, or parameter value recorded at the time of the violation (e.g., turbidity in NTU, chlorine residual in mg/L, BOD in mg/L, CT value in mg·min/L). This is the quantitative result that triggered the violation.']
  measured_value_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the measured_value field (e.g., mg/L for chemical concentrations, NTU for Nephelometric Turbidity Units, mg·min/L for CT Contact Time, CFU/100mL for microbial counts). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg/L|NTU|mg·min/L|CFU/100mL|pH|mg/kg|ug/L|pCi/L|MPN/100mL — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mor_reporting_period varchar [note: 'The MOR reporting period (YYYY-MM) in which this violation must be included for regulatory submission. Links the violation to the corresponding Monthly Operating Report for the facility.']
  operator_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the certified treatment plant operator to record observations, circumstances, and contextual information about the violation at the time of detection or during investigation.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty assessed by the regulatory agency for this violation, in USD. Supports financial liability tracking, OPEX reporting, and GAAP/GASB financial disclosures for municipal utilities.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'The operating permit number (e.g., NPDES permit number, state drinking water permit) under which the facility operates and against which the violation is recorded. Used in regulatory correspondence and DMR/MOR submissions.']
  public_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which public notification was issued to customers and the public in accordance with the EPA Public Notification Rule. Null if notification has not yet been issued.']
  public_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation triggers a mandatory public notification requirement under EPA SDWA Public Notification Rule (40 CFR Part 141 Subpart Q). True when the violation type and severity mandate customer and public notification.']
  public_notification_tier varchar [note: 'EPA Public Notification Rule tier classification: Tier 1 requires notification within 24 hours (acute health risk); Tier 2 requires notification within 30 days; Tier 3 requires annual notification. Drives notification workflow and deadline management.']
  regulatory_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The applicable Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL), treatment technique standard, or permit limit that was exceeded or violated. For CT violations, this is the required CT value in mg·min/L.']
  regulatory_limit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory standard that was violated: MCL (Maximum Contaminant Level), MCLG (Maximum Contaminant Level Goal), treatment technique standard, NPDES permit limit, action level (e.g., Lead and Copper Rule), or required CT (Contact Time for Disinfection).']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory agency (EPA or State Primacy Agency) was formally notified of this violation, as required by SDWA or NPDES permit conditions.']
  regulatory_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the formal notification to the regulatory agency (EPA or State Primacy Agency) regarding this violation. Tracks whether notification is pending, has been submitted, has been acknowledged, or is overdue per regulatory deadlines.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the violation was resolved and compliance was restored, confirmed by a return-to-compliance sample result or regulatory acknowledgment. Null if the violation remains open.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the root cause of the violation to support trend analysis and preventive action programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|process_upset|operator_error|source_water_event|chemical_dosing|monitoring_failure|reporting_failure|infrastructure|other — promote to reference product]']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Assessed severity classification of the violation based on the degree of exceedance, public health risk, and regulatory consequence. Critical indicates immediate public health risk; high indicates significant exceedance; medium indicates moderate concern; low indicates minor or administrative violation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this violation record was last modified in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and incremental Silver Layer processing.']
  violation_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the treatment violation or regulatory exceedance was first identified or occurred, as recorded in the compliance management system.']
  violation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number assigned to this violation record, used in regulatory correspondence, public notifications, and compliance tracking systems.']
  violation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the violation record, tracking its progression from identification through corrective action to regulatory closure.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory violation category. MCL exceedance indicates a Maximum Contaminant Level was exceeded; treatment technique indicates a required process was not followed; monitoring and reporting indicates a missed sample or late report; CT violation indicates Contact Time for disinfection was insufficient; turbidity violation indicates NTU limits were breached. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mcl_exceedance|treatment_technique|monitoring_reporting|ct_violation|turbidity_violation|public_notification|other — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records treatment-related regulatory violations and exceedances at WTP/WWTP facilities. Captures violation type (MCL exceedance, treatment technique violation, monitoring and reporting violation, CT violation, turbidity violation), violation date, contaminant or parameter, measured value, regulatory limit, severity level, public notification requirement triggered, corrective action plan, resolution date, and regulatory agency notification status. Supports compliance management and public notification workflows.'
}
